en.extract:
The Philippine pied fantail (Rhipidura nigritorquis) is a medium-size species of bird in the family Rhipiduridae endemic to the Philippines.  It was formerly considered conspecific with the Malaysian pied fantail.


Description and taxonomy
This bird is of moderate size and has a long tail. It is commonly found in wooded areas and open spaces in lowland regions. Its upper body is a sooty gray color, while its underbody is white. It features a black band on its chest and a black face, with a distinct white throat and brow. It frequently moves its tail from side to side, displaying its white tips. The bird's song is composed of diverse, somewhat unmusical, and scratchy whistled phrases.
Other names by which this bird is referred to are Maria Capra (Philippines), and tarerekoy (Visayas, Philippines).

It differs from the Oriental magpie-robin with its white belly, all black tail, paler gray throat and breast in female, smaller size especially its bill and wing. It also considerably differs vocally. 


Ecology and behavior
The Philippine pied fantail eats insects, which it catches on the wing. It joins mixed flocks with other insectivorous birds, and sometimes follows large mammals to feed on insects they disturb.
It breeds from March to June, nesting in a cup made from grass, roots, and fibres lined with spider webs. Clutch size is usually 2 to 3 eggs. 
It is known to be very aggressive against potential predators, diving on humans, cats, and dogs when breeding


Habitat and conservation status
This species is extremely adaptable to a variety of wooded and scrubby lowland habitats, found in lowland forest, second growth, agricultural areas, scrub, beaches, and parks.  The IUCN has classified the species as least concern as it is common throughout its wide range, though they do evaluate its population as declining.


References

The '''Philippine pied fantail''' ('''''Rhipidura nigritorquis''''') is a medium-size species of [[bird]] in the family [[Rhipiduridae]] endemic to the Philippines.  It was formerly considered conspecific with the [[Malaysian pied fantail]].

== Description and taxonomy ==

This bird is of moderate size and has a long tail. It is commonly found in wooded areas and open spaces in lowland regions. Its upper body is a sooty gray color, while its underbody is white. It features a black band on its chest and a black face, with a distinct white throat and brow. It frequently moves its tail from side to side, displaying its white tips. The bird's song is composed of diverse, somewhat unmusical, and scratchy whistled phrases.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Philippine Pied Fantail - eBird |url=https://ebird.org/species/phipif1 |access-date=2023-06-12 |website=ebird.org |language=en}}</ref>

Other names by which this bird is referred to are Maria Capra (Philippines),<ref>[http://birdsofmakati.blogspot.com/ Birds of Makati], ''Blog''</ref> and tarerekoy ([[Visayas]], Philippines).<ref>Kennedy, Robert S. ''A Guide to the Birds of the Philippines''</ref>

It differs from the [[Oriental magpie-robin]] with its white belly, all black tail, paler gray throat and breast in female, smaller size especially its bill and wing. It also considerably differs vocally. <ref>{{Cite journal |last1=del Hoyo |first1=Josep |last2=Collar |first2=Nigel |last3=Christie |first3=David |date=2020 |title=Philippine Magpie-Robin (Copsychus mindanensis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/phimar1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.phimar1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>[[File:KIRKAMON-PHILIPPINE PIED FANTAIL 2.jpg|left|thumb|205x205px|On a nest]]

== Ecology and behavior ==
The Philippine pied fantail eats insects, which it catches [[Hawking (birds)|on the wing]]. It joins mixed flocks with other insectivorous birds, and sometimes follows large mammals to feed on insects they disturb.<ref name="birds-of-the-world">{{cite book |last1=del Hoyo |first1=Josep |last2=Elliott |first2=Andrew |last3=Christie |first3=David |title=Handbook of the Birds of the World: Volume 11: Old World Flycatchers to Old World Warblers |date=2006 |publisher=Lynx Edicions |location=Barcelona |isbn=84-96553-06-X |page=237}}</ref>

It breeds from March to June, nesting in a cup made from grass, roots, and fibres lined with spider webs. Clutch size is usually 2 to 3 eggs. 

It is known to be very aggressive against potential predators, diving on humans, cats, and dogs when breeding<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=del Hoyo |first1=Josep |last2=Collar |first2=Nigel |last3=Christie |first3=David |date=2020 |title=Philippine Magpie-Robin (Copsychus mindanensis), version 1.0 |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/phimar1/1.0/introduction |journal=Birds of the World |language=en |doi=10.2173/bow.phimar1.01 |issn=2771-3105|url-access=subscription }}</ref>

== Habitat and conservation status ==
This species is extremely adaptable to a variety of wooded and scrubby lowland habitats, found in lowland forest, second growth, agricultural areas, scrub, beaches, and parks.  The [[IUCN]] has classified the species as [[Least Concern|least concern]] as it is common throughout its wide range, though they do evaluate its population as declining.<ref>{{Cite report |title=Rhipidura nigritorquis: BirdLife International: The IUCN Red List of Threatened Species 2024: e.T103709532A264332458 |last=IUCN |date=2016-10-01 |publisher=International Union for Conservation of Nature |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T103709532A264332458.en |language=en}}</ref>

zh.extract:
菲律宾斑扇尾鹟（学名：Rhipidura nigritorquis），是雀形目扇尾鹟科的一种鸟类。


特征
菲律宾斑扇尾鹟体重约17克，翼长约68毫米，嘴峰长约14毫米，喙宽度约4.5毫米，喙厚度约3.7毫米，跗蹠长约16.1毫米，尾长约67.3毫米。菲律宾斑扇尾鹟在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在半开放的中等高度的林地中，食性为肉食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


分布
菲律宾群岛和苏禄群岛。
